summary: Unsafe SQL query construction using string interpolation enables SQL injection.

findings.title: SQL query constructed with unsanitized user input
findings.evidence: Function get_user builds the query as "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '%s'" % username, directly interpolating the username into the SQL string.
findings.impact: An attacker controlling the username parameter can inject arbitrary SQL into the WHERE clause, potentially bypassing authentication, reading sensitive data, or modifying database contents.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries instead of string interpolation, for example: q = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?"; return conn.execute(q, (username,)); additionally validate username against an expected pattern before executing the query.
